AgentPeek is a specialized monitoring tool designed specifically for users of Claude Code and Codex AI assistants, providing real-time insights directly from the Mac notch. Its core purpose is to offer developers and AI enthusiasts a seamless way to track and manage their AI sessions, permissions, token usage, and local development servers without compromising privacy or requiring cloud-based synchronization. By leveraging the Mac notch as a discreet yet accessible interface, AgentPeek integrates smoothly into the Apple silicon Mac ecosystem, delivering a native experience that is both efficient and secure. At its heart, AgentPeek focuses on transparency and control. results.title: AgentPeek Review 2026: Pricing, Features, Alternatives | Mindber
results.content:
Problem Solved

## What problem does AgentPeek solve?

It's for developers who use Claude Code or Codex in the terminal and need to keep an eye on sessions, permissions, and local servers without constantly switching windows. Today they have to manually check terminal tabs or miss prompts and permission requests, which slows down their flow. AgentPeek surfaces live sessions, pending questions, and dev servers right in the Mac notch so you can respond or jump to a folder with one click. For example, a developer running Claude Code to refactor auth middleware can see the session status, answer a permission prompt, and open the project folder without leaving their editor.

Company & Maker

## Requirements

## Privacy

AgentNotch runs 100% locally. No data is sent anywhere — it only receives telemetry from your local AI tools.

## License

MIT License — see LICENSE for details.

Made with ❤️ for developers who love AI coding assistants

## About

AgentNotch is a sleek macOS menu bar app that lives in your Mac's notch, providing real-time visibility into your AI coding assistants. Watch as Claude Code and OpenAI Codex think, read files, and execute tools — all without leaving your editor.

## What is AgentNotch?

AgentNotch is a sleek macOS menu bar app that lives in your Mac's notch, providing real-time visibility into your AI coding assistants. Watch as Claude Code and OpenAI Codex think, read files, and execute tools — all without leaving your editor.

## Features

### 🎯 Real-Time Tool Tracking

See every tool call as it happens — file reads, code edits, shell commands, and more. Know exactly what your AI assistant is doing at any moment.

### 📊 Token & Cost Monitoring

Track token usage (input/output) and estimated costs in real-time. Never be surprised by API bills again.

### 🎨 Source-Aware Design

Visual distinction lets you know which AI is active at a glance.

### 🔔 Completion Detection [...] ### 🔔 Completion Detection

Get notified when your AI assistant finishes a task. No more wondering "is it still thinking?"

### ⚡ Lightweight & Native

### ⚙️ Configurable

## Installation

### Homebrew (Recommended)

### Manual Download

`AgentNotch-1.0.0.zip`
`AgentNotch.app`
`/Applications`

## Usage

### Setup with Claude Code

Add to your Claude Code configuration to send telemetry:

### Setup with Codex

AgentNotch listens for OTLP/HTTP on port 4318 by default, and currently decodes OTLP logs (`/v1/logs`) and metrics (`/v1/metrics`).

`/v1/logs`
`/v1/metrics`

Codex CLI (v0.79+) uses `~/.codex/config.toml` with an `[otel]` section (not `[telemetry]`). Add:

`~/.codex/config.toml`
`[otel]`
`[telemetry]`
